div.h2: Incremental HTTP Parser: Accumulation Across Multiple Read Events
div.p: Parser is stateless between calls — always scans from buf[0]; resumability is free from the accumulation model

g.text: Scenario A — Headers Arrive in Three Chunks
Scenario B — Terminator Spans Chunk Boundary

div.p:
Edge case: the  of 

 arrives at the end of Event 2;
the 

 arrives at the start of Event 3.
find_header_end() correctly finds it only after Event 3.
Why it works without sub-state: Parser rescans from buf[0] on every call. No saved "last byte seen" pointer needed. The scan cost is O(read_len) per event — acceptable at 16KB max.
read_buf[0..399] — 400 bytes valid GET /index.html HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: localh...

g.text: PARSE_INCOMPLETE → return, wait for more data
PARSE_INCOMPLETE → return, wait for more data

div.p: read_buf[0..849] — 850 bytes valid ...Connection: keep-alive\r\n\r\n ← terminator at offset 840

g.text: Scans all accumulated bytes; '\r\n\r' at tail — no '\n' yet
Full \r\n\r\n now in buffer at correct offset — PARSE_COMPLETE
